subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as

Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Benzene is more stable than hypothetical cyclohexatriene.

Reason R : The delocalized π electron cloud is attracted more strongly by nuclei of carbon atoms.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as

Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Benzene is more stable than hypothetical cyclohexatriene.

Reason R : The delocalized π electron cloud is attracted more strongly by nuclei of carbon atoms.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
solution: The delocalised π-electron cloud is attracted more strongly by the nuclei of the carbon atoms than the electron cloud localised between two carbon atoms. Therefore, presence of delocalised π-electrons in benzene makes it more stable than the hypothetical cyclohexatriene.

q:

CH3CH24CH3→HCl△Anhy.AlCl3Xmajor product
question:
In the following reaction ‘X’ is

CH3CH24CH3→HCl△Anhy.AlCl3Xmajor product
solution:
When n-Hexane is heated with anhydrous AlCl3 and HCl gas at 573K under a pressure of about 30-35atm it Isomerises to give a branched chain alkanes.

CH3CH24CH3→HCl△Anhy.AlCl3CH32CHCH2CH2CH32-methylpentane+3-methylpentane

Major product in the above reaction is 2-methylpentane.

On ozonolysis, 'X' yields two moles of methane dicarbaldehyde. The hydrocarbon 'X' is :
question: A hydrocarbon 'X' with formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>8</mn></msub></math> uses two moles of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> on catalystic hydrogenation of its one mole. On ozonolysis, 'X' yields two moles of methane dicarbaldehyde. The hydrocarbon 'X' is :
solution: A hydrocarbon X with formula C6H8 uses two moles of H2 on catalystic hydrogenation of its one mole. On ozonolysis,  X yields two moles of methane dicarbaldehyde. The hydrocarbon is cyclohexa-1,4-diene.

A. p-xylene

B. bromobenzene

C. mesitylene

D. nitrobenzene

E. benzene

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
Nitration is an electrophilic aromatic substitution reaction. The +M effect increases the rate of nitration whereas the -M effect decreases the rate of nitration.

 -NO2(-M), -Br(+M but dominant -I) and -CH3(+H)

-NO2 is strongly deactivating

-Br ,  deactivating

-CH3,  activating group

D<B<E<A<C

subject: Chemistry
q: The major product obtained in the given reaction is:
question: The major product obtained in the given reaction is:
solution:
We know that AlCl3 is a lewis acid. It can give Friedel Crafts reactions. Here, we can see that AlCl3 acts as a lewis acid and accepts the electron pair from the chlorine atom. This forms−AlCl4. Alongside this, a carbocation is also formed.

Now, this carbocation is an electrophilic carbon. So, benzene rings can give electrophilic substitution reactions here. So, as a result, the double bond of benzene will attack on the carbocation and a C-C bond will be formed there. Then after losing a proton, the carbon ring becomes aromatic. This, we obtain a compound which has two ring structures. One ring is aromatic and the other is not aromatic.

Friedel-Craft alkylation takes place to give a six membered ring.

During an electrophilic addition reactions double bonds donate lone pair electrons to an electrophile.

Markovnikov's Rule: The addition of a proton acid to the double bond of an alkene results in a product with the acid hydrogen bound to the carbon atom that already has the greater number of hydrogen attached.

q: In the presence of sunlight, benzene reacts with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> to give product, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>. The number of hydrogens in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> is
question: In the presence of sunlight, benzene reacts with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> to give product, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>. The number of hydrogens in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> is
solution: In the presence of ultraviolet light (but without a catalyst present), hot benzene will also undergo an addition reaction with chlorine. The ring delocalisation is permanently broken, and a chlorine atom adds on to each carbon atom. 1,2,3,4,5,6-hexachlorocyclohexane or benzenehexachloride is formed as a product. It is an example of free radical addition reaction.

subject: Chemistry
q: What will be the major product in the following mononitration reaction?
question: What will be the major product in the following mononitration reaction?
solution: Addition of NO2 is an electrophilic addition. The N increases the electron density on the left benzene ring, whereas the carbonyl group decreases the charge density of the right benzene group. As a result, NO2 is added to the left benzene group due to higher charge density.

q:
Consider the following reactions:
a CH33CCHOHCH3→conc.H2SO4
b CH32CHCHBrCH3→alc.KOH
c CH32CHCHBrCH3→CH33O⊖K⊕
d

Which of the reaction(s) will not produce Saytzeff product?
question:
Consider the following reactions:
a CH33CCHOHCH3→conc.H2SO4
b CH32CHCHBrCH3→alc.KOH
c CH32CHCHBrCH3→CH33O⊖K⊕
d

Which of the reaction(s) will not produce Saytzeff product?
solution: We have to know that a Saytzeff product is basically an alkene compound which has undergone many substitution reactions to form the alkene product. From Saytzeff rule, a stable alkene is formed when removal of hydrogen from beta carbon has a low number of hydrogen atoms.

subject: Chemistry
q: Total number of deactivating groups in aromatic electrophilic substitution reaction among the following is
question: Total number of deactivating groups in aromatic electrophilic substitution reaction among the following is
solution:
Atoms or groups that make the benzene molecule more reactive by increasing the ring's electron density are called activating groups.

An atom or group that makes the benzene molecule less reactive by removing electron density from the ring acts as a deactivating group. Deactivating groups direct incoming electrophiles to the meta position. Deactivating groups decreases the rate of reaction relative to H. If the atom of the group attached to benzene ring has pi bond then that group act as deactivating group.

Hence,  -CN, -COCH3 are deactivating groups.

subject: Chemistry
q: 0.5 g of an organic compound on combustion gave 1.46 g of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and 0.9 g of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. The percentage of carbon in the compound is _______. (Nearest integer) [Given : Molar mass (in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$) $\mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{H}: 1$, O: 16]
question: 0.5 g of an organic compound on combustion gave 1.46 g of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and 0.9 g of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. The percentage of carbon in the compound is _______. (Nearest integer) [Given : Molar mass (in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$) $\mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{H}: 1$, O: 16]
solution:
Organic $\rightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2$
Compound
Applying POAC on ' C '
(mole) of ' C ' in compound $=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{CO}_2} \times 1$
So mass of ' C ' in compound
$=\frac{1.46}{44} \times 12$
So, $\%$ of ' C ' in compound $=\frac{1.46}{44} \times \frac{12}{0.5} \times 100$
$=79.63$

transPh-CH=CH-CH3→ cis Ph-CH=CH-CH3
question:
Choose the correct set of reagents for the following conversion
transPh-CH=CH-CH3→ cis Ph-CH=CH-CH3
solution:
A trans alkene can be converted to a vicinal dihalide through a halogenation reaction. The vicinal dihalide can undergo double elimination to afford an alkyne. The alkyne can undergo partial reduction to give a cis alkene.

The reagents needed to convert trans(Ph - CH = CH - CH3) to cis-(Ph - CH = CH - CH3) are shown in the reaction scheme below.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which among the following pairs of the structures will give different products on ozonolysis? (Consider the double bonds in the structures are rigid and not delocalized.)
question: Which among the following pairs of the structures will give different products on ozonolysis? (Consider the double bonds in the structures are rigid and not delocalized.)
solution:
Ozone is a very reactive allotrope of oxygen. The reaction of ozone with alkenes and alkynes causes the oxidative cleaving of the alkene or alkyne. The carbon-carbon double bonds are replaced with carbon-oxygen double bonds, giving the required carbonyl product as shown below.

In all other options both compounds will give the same product on ozonolysis.
